headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Falls As Oil Prices Rise, Trump Extends Ceasefire; These Gold Miners Fall (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes weakened in the final minutes of trading Tuesday as President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran.  Meanwhile, Northern Trust sprinted higher as gold names slid on the stock market today.  The ceasefire was set to expire on Wednesday, but Trump stretched the halt to an undefined time saying Tehran's government was "seriously fractured."

headline: UnitedHealth beats profit expectations, on track for turnaround
summary: STORY: Shares of UnitedHealth jumped more than 10% on Tuesday after the company raised its annual profit forecast and beat first-quarter earnings estimates.::UnitedhealthcareThe results showed a notable turnaround from last May, when the nation's largest health insurer pulled its financial outlook and its CEO stepped down, as unexpected demand by members for medical services dented profits.Former CEO Stephen Hemsley, who had led the insurer from 2006 to 2017, returned to the top job, looking to rebuild investor confidence by keeping costs in check.He also sought to reassure employees after the killing of a UnitedHealth senior executive in late 2024 led to a torrent of criticism of health insurance practices.Over the last year, UnitedHealth has exited non-U.S. businesses and some health plans amid an industry-wide surge in costs due to increased demand from Medicare patients.:: FileAs part of its turnaround plan, UnitedHealth also reshuffled leadership roles, and on Tuesday said it will spend $1.5 billion on AI.The results also helped boost shares of its rivals, with CVS Health and Humana gaining as much as 3% and 6%, respectively.

raw.headline: UnitedHealth beats profit expectations, on track for turnaround
raw.summary: STORY: Shares of UnitedHealth jumped more than 10% on Tuesday after the company raised its annual profit forecast and beat first-quarter earnings estimates.::UnitedhealthcareThe results showed a notable turnaround from last May, when the nation's largest health insurer pulled its financial outlook and its CEO stepped down, as unexpected demand by members for medical services dented profits.Former CEO Stephen Hemsley, who had led the insurer from 2006 to 2017, returned to the top job, looking to rebuild investor confidence by keeping costs in check.He also sought to reassure employees after the killing of a UnitedHealth senior executive in late 2024 led to a torrent of criticism of health insurance practices.Over the last year, UnitedHealth has exited non-U.S. businesses and some health plans amid an industry-wide surge in costs due to increased demand from Medicare patients.:: FileAs part of its turnaround plan, UnitedHealth also reshuffled leadership roles, and on Tuesday said it will spend $1.5 billion on AI.The results also helped boost shares of its rivals, with CVS Health and Humana gaining as much as 3% and 6%, respectively.

headline: Stocks Turn Lower. Iran Hasn't Confirmed It Will Attend Peace Talks.
summary: Stocks took a dive after Pakistan's information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said Iran has not provided a formal confirmation its delegation will attend peace talks in Pakistan.  The Dow was down 103 points, or 0.2%.  "Pakistan has made sincere efforts to convince the Iranian leadership to participate in the second round of talks and these efforts continue," Tarar said.

headline: Nexben Transforms Health Benefits Through New Leadership, Names Distinguished Healthcare Executive as CEO
summary: Former UnitedHealth senior leader, and operating partner at Chicago Pacific Founders, Mark Smith, joins Nexben as CEO to put health benefits back into the hands of consumers. Positioned at the intersection of healthcare and fintech, the organization's ICHRA solution enables employers to provide benefits with predictable spend while offering customizable plans for employees. Nexben's unique technology also serves brokers, carriers, and enterprise partners.

headline: UnitedHealth Leads Dow Jones As Earnings Heal, Membership Falls
summary: UnitedHealth Group blew past low earnings expectations as health costs came in much lower than forecast as the number of insured members fell.  Shares of the embattled health giant, which recently reclaimed a key long-term technical level, strode higher early Tuesday, boosting the Dow Jones Industrial Average and peers such as Elevance Health, Humana and CVS Health.  Medical costs as a share of premiums fell to 83.9% in Q1, falling 90 basis points from a year ago and undercutting 85.5% forecasts.

headline: Stocks and oil prices hold relatively steady in the countdown to US-Iran ceasefire talks
summary: U.S. stocks are holding firm Tuesday after UnitedHealth Group and other big companies showed they’re making even bigger profits than analysts expected.  Oil prices, meanwhile, remained relatively stable as optimism seems to be sticking in financial markets that the United States and Iran will avoid a worst-case scenario for the economy, even with their war ongoing.  UnitedHealth helped lead the market with a jump of 9.1% after reporting stronger profit and revenue for the beginning of the year than analysts expected.
